en: Why don't I know you?
I'm new. I just moved here from Africa.
What? I used to be homeschooled.
Wait, what? My mom taught me at home.
ko: 잠깐, 뭐라고? 우리 엄마가 집에서 가르쳤어.

en: No, no, I know what homeschool is. I'm not retarded.
ko: 아니, 아니, 홈스쿨이 뭔지 알아. 나 바보 아니야.

en: So you've actually never been to a real school before?
ko: 그럼 진짜 학교에 가본 적이 없는 거야?

en: Shut up.
Shut up!
I didn't say anything.
Homeschooled. That's really interesting. Thanks. You're, like, really pretty.
ko: 홈스쿨링. 정말 흥미롭네. 고마워. 너 정말 예쁘다.

en: Thank you. You agree. What? You think you're really pretty.
ko: 고마워. 너도 동의해. 뭐? 너도 정말 예쁘다고 생각해?

en: Oh, I don't know.
Oh my god, I love your bracelet. Where did you get it?
ko: 오 마이 갓, 너의 팔찌 너무 예쁘다. 어디서 샀어?

en: Oh, my mom made it for me. It's adorable.
It's so fetch. What is fetch?
Oh, it's like slang. From England.
ko: 아, 그건 속어야. 영국에서 온 거야.

en: So if you're from Africa, why are you white?
Oh my god, Karen, you can't just ask people why they're white.
ko: 오 마이 갓, 카렌, 사람들에게 왜 백인인지 물어보면 안 돼.

en: Could you give us some privacy for, like, one second?
ko: 우리에게 사생활을 좀 줄 수 있어? 한 순간만?

en: Yeah, sure.
Okay, we should just know that we don't do this a lot, so this is, like, a really huge deal.
ko: 좋아, 우리는 이런 걸 자주 하지 않으니까, 이건 정말 큰 일이야.
